name: Mr T Thailand
fixture_purpose: UK couple at Decumulation life stage (both age 68 — anchor archetype 52). Planning move to Thailand within 12 months. LTR (Long Term Resident) visa approved under Wealthy Pensioner category. Exercises: UK tax residency cessation projection and split-year treatment; LTR visa passive income requirement (THB 80k/month met); post-arrival Thai tax residency threshold (180 days); LTR visa 10-year foreign income tax exemption (principal benefit of Wealthy Pensioner category); UK SIPP drawdown remittance strategy (pre/post Thailand's 2024 remittance rule change for foreign-source income); UK main residence sold (PPR exempt); Thai property purchased (THB 25m); SSF/RMF eligibility gates post-arrival. FX baseline: THB 44 per GBP. Tax bundle: TH-2026.1 (stub-OPEN at fixture date — engine must gracefully handle incomplete Thai rules).

jurisdiction.tax_bundle_secondary_status: stub-OPEN at fixture date; engine must apply conservative UK-only calc with TH annotations; display TH-rules-pending marker on affected outputs
jurisdiction.domicile_note: UK domicile of origin; not changing; relevant to UK IHT position even post-departure

ltr_10yr_foreign_income_exemption.exemption_scope: All foreign-source income earned before Thai residency AND remitted to Thailand exempt from Thai income tax for 10 years from visa approval date
ltr_10yr_foreign_income_exemption.engine_note: LTR Wealthy Pensioner category exempts foreign income from Thai tax for 10 years; no Thai income tax on UK pension or investment income remitted; engine marks TH-side income as tax-exempt under LTR until 2036

split_year_cases_applicable: Case 4 — Leaving UK to work full-time overseas
split_year_treatment.note: Case 4 (or Case 1 for overseas work) — HMRC split-year treatment applies; UK-part income taxed in UK; overseas-part income (from 1 Apr 2027) taxed in Thai resident regime under LTR exemption
split_year_treatment.engine_note: Split-year treatment requires dual-period tax calc; engine must separate UK-part and overseas-part for 2026/27

uk_srt_post_departure.note: After departure, if visiting UK and spending >90 days/yr for 5+ yrs could re-trigger UK residency; engine should note threshold

remittance_to_thailand.post_departure_strategy: draw and remit as needed for Thai living costs
remittance_to_thailand.uk_tax_post_departure: not UK tax resident; UK SIPP drawdown NOT subject to UK income tax once non-UK resident UNLESS double taxation treaty provision applies
remittance_to_thailand.dtaa_note: UK-Thailand DTA: Article 17 (Pensions) — government pensions taxable only in UK; private pensions (SIPP) taxable in country of residence; post-departure SIPP draw = taxable in Thailand ONLY; exempt under LTR for 10 years

investments.isa_post_departure_note: Cannot contribute to ISA once non-UK resident; subscriptions allowed up to departure date in split year; existing ISA can be retained and grows tax-free but no new subscriptions

investments.post_departure_note: CGT on GIA disposals post-departure: UK non-resident; no UK CGT on most asset disposals EXCEPT UK residential property; GIA gains likely exempt from UK CGT once non-resident

property.ppr_relief_note: Full PPR relief — sole main residence throughout ownership; zero chargeable gain

property.thai_property_ownership_note: Foreigners cannot own freehold land in Thailand; condo purchased under Condominium Act (up to 49% foreign quota); freehold unit title held; land component nil
property.uk_iht_note: Thai property NOT UK situs; UK domicile individuals taxed on worldwide assets; Thai condo is in IHT estate as non-UK situs asset — different from non-dom

bank.note: Holding proceeds from UK property sale pending Thailand move; to be drawn down as Thai living expenses build
Opened for property purchase transaction; will become main spending account post-departure

protection.policy_portability_note: UK life policy continues post-departure; premiums payable from UK bank account; insurer notified of relocation — agreed to continue coverage

gifts_and_trusts.purpose: Inheritance advance — pre-move gift; 7-year clock starts now
gifts_and_trusts.note: Gifting to reduce UK estate before IHT position changes on FA2026 activation; 7-year clock must be survived for PET to fall out of estate

events.note: SIPP enters IHT estate on this date; both individual and partner SIPPs affected; cost of inaction calculation critical

fp4.expected_behaviour: TH-2026.1 bundle stub-OPEN; engine applies UK-only calc; TH-side outputs annotated 'TH rules pending — provisional'; LTR exemption hardcoded as confirmed; SSF/RMF cannot be modelled until bundle complete

Thai property value is purchase-price-as-valuation; no independent valuation post-purchase; FX rate applied at fixture date (THB 44/GBP); GBP value fluctuates with exchange rate; engine flags FX staleness if >30 days

Split-year 2026/27 requires HMRC Case 4 determination; engine models provisionally; actual case determination depends on circumstances at year-end; IHT and income tax modelled pre/post departure separately

fp5.expected_behaviour: UK-Thailand DTA Article 17: private pension income taxable only in resident country post-departure; LTR exempts for 10 years; engine marks SIPP drawdown post-departure as Thailand-taxable but LTR-exempt; flag when LTR approaches expiry 2036

Cannot contribute to ISA post-departure as non-UK resident; engine generates APQ: maximise ISA contributions in 2026/27 split year up to departure date; existing ISA retained and continues tax-free wrapper but frozen subscription

pension_remittance_strategy.optimal_draw_timing: draw before LTR exemption expires where possible; review post-2036 when Thai tax will apply

thailand_2024_rule_change.old_rule: Foreign income only taxable in Thailand if remitted in same year as earned
thailand_2024_rule_change.new_rule_from_2024: All foreign-source income assessable in year of remittance regardless of year earned
thailand_2024_rule_change.note: LTR Wealthy Pensioner exemption protects from 2024 rule change for duration of LTR; critical planning consideration post-LTR expiry

ssf_rmf_consideration.ltr_exemption_interaction: If income exempt under LTR, SSF/RMF relief may be academic (no tax to offset) — specialist Thai tax advice required post-arrival
ssf_rmf_consideration.engine_note: Cannot model SSF/RMF until TH bundle complete and Thai residency status active

finio_score.confidence_reason: TH tax bundle stub; split-year treatment not yet triggered; FA2026 SIPP IHT activation approaching; FX exposure; otherwise well-planned transition

behaviour.note: Pre-move gifting started; ISA maximising; pension nominations current

capital.note: Substantial combined wealth; diversified across pensions, ISAs, Thai property, GIA

tax.note: TH bundle stub limits confidence; LTR exemption confirmed but SSF/RMF modelling pending; split-year complexity

protection.note: Joint whole-of-life in trust; policy portable to Thailand; no liabilities

cashflow.note: Pensions and state pension provide stable income well above LTR requirement; living costs in Thailand lower than UK estimate

debt.note: No liabilities; Thai property cash purchase; clean balance sheet

estate.note: FA2026 activation April 2027 adds £1.1m to IHT estate; UK domicile means worldwide assets in estate; Thai property in estate; IHT planning urgently required

net_worth.confidence_reason: Thai property FX-sensitive; TH bundle stub; ISA and SIPP valuations current
net_worth.note: Includes: SIPPs £1.1m + ISAs £330k + GIA £85k + Thai property £568k + UK cash £145k + Thai bank £20k + Premium Bonds £100k. UK main residence sold Feb 2026.

iht_projection.confidence_reason: FA2026 activation date confirmed (6 Apr 2027); estate calculation dependent on valuation at second death; PETs reduce estate if survived 7 years
iht_projection.note: UK domicile = worldwide assets in IHT estate including Thai condo

cost_of_inaction.explanation: FA2026 activation 6 April 2027 — 348 days away at fixture date. Combined SIPPs £1.1m enter IHT estate at 40% = £440k additional IHT. PET programme started (£100k gifted) but 7-year survival required. Mitigation: accelerate drawdown, increase gifts, consider trust structuring before departure.
